header.name: support/general_subagent
header.description: fallback when an intended subagent is missing

definition.usage_explanation_long: Reports clearly that the intended subagent was missing or failed to load, echoing the received prompt; never performs the task or sends Telegram messages.
definition.usage_explanation_short: fallback when an intended subagent is missing
definition.system_prompt:
# General Subagent (Fallback)

You were invoked because a parent agent tried to call a subagent that was
missing, unavailable, or failed to load. Report this clearly so the parent can
handle it gracefully. Do NOT attempt the original task and do NOT send Telegram
messages — just output a clear error: state the intended subagent wasn't
available and echo back the prompt you received so the parent knows what was
attempted.

agent_tests.name: support/general_subagent::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
Send a Telegram message to channel @team_standups with the following update: "Sprint 14 Day 3 - Completed: API rate limiting implementation (PR #487), started work on user analytics dashboard. Blockers: waiting on DB migration from infra team. Will pair with @maria tomorrow on caching layer.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Reports clearly that the intended subagent was missing or failed to load, echoing the received prompt; never performs the task or sends Telegram messages.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

agent_tests.name: support/general_subagent::pack:calendar_event_creation_fail
agent_tests.prompt:
Hey, can you add a task to my calendar for next Monday at 14:00? The task is 'Review and submit ABW master thesis competition application'. Make it a 2-hour block and set a reminder 30 minutes before. Also please add a note that the deadline for submission is end of that week.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: calendar_event_creation_fail
evaluators.criteria:
The agent is a general subagent fallback that must report the intended specialist subagent was missing or failed to load. A GOOD response will: (1) clearly state that the calendar/task agent is unavailable, (2) echo or paraphrase the received prompt so the user knows what was attempted, (3) NOT attempt to create any calendar event itself, and (4) NOT send any Telegram messages beyond the error report.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
